agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation is a core lifecycle operation in logistics and courier systems. A user-initiated cancellation must respect the physical realities of fulfillment — once a package is in transit or delivered, cancellation becomes increasingly complex or impossible. The PM statement 'Users should be able to cancel their order' is underspecified across multiple critical dimensions: which order states allow cancellation, refund policy, partial cancellation, and who qualifies as 'user'.","bounded_context":"Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Order","definition":"A request by a user for pickup and delivery of goods, tracked through a defined lifecycle of states."},{"term":"Order Cancellation","definition":"A user-initiated action that terminates an active order before final delivery, potentially triggering refund and operational rollback processes."},{"term":"Order State","definition":"The current phase of an order in its lifecycle (e.g., Placed, Confirmed, Assigned, PickedUp, InTransit, Delivered, Cancelled)."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The set of order states during which cancellation is permitted without penalty or with defined penalties."},{"term":"Refund","definition":"The return of payment to the user following a cancellation, subject to policy rules based on order state at time of cancellation."},{"term":"Cancellation Fee","definition":"A charge deducted from the refund amount when cancellation occurs after a courier has been assigned or has begun work."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"An order may only be cancelled when it is in a cancellable state. Once an order reaches a terminal state (Delivered, already Cancelled), cancellation is not permitted.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing cancellation of a delivered or already-cancelled order violates physical reality and state machine integrity. A delivered package cannot be un-delivered through cancellation.","testable_assertion":"Given an order in state Delivered or Cancelled, when a cancellation request is made, then the system must reject it with a clear error."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Only the user who placed the order may cancel it.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing arbitrary users to cancel other users' orders is a data integrity and authorization violation.","testable_assertion":"Given a cancellation request from user B for an order placed by user A, then the system must reject it with an authorization error."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A cancelled order must transition to the Cancelled state and remain there permanently. Cancelled is a terminal state.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Reopening a cancelled order would corrupt downstream accounting, courier scheduling, and audit trails.","testable_assertion":"Given an order that has been cancelled, when any state transition is attempted, then the system must reject it."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"When an order is cancelled, any assigned courier must be notified and unassigned.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Notification timing and mechanism are operational policy, but the business intent is clear — a courier must not continue work on a cancelled order.","testable_assertion":"Given an order in Assigned or PickedUp state that is cancelled, then the assigned courier receives a cancellation notification and is released from the assignment."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"A cancellation must record the timestamp, initiating user, order state at time of cancellation, and reason (if provided) for audit purposes.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Audit trail integrity is a hard system requirement for dispute resolution and financial reconciliation.","testable_assertion":"Given a successful cancellation, then a cancellation record exists with timestamp, user ID, prior order state, and optional reason."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An order exists in state Placed or Confirmed (before courier assignment)","when":"The order owner requests cancellation","then":"The order transitions to Cancelled, a full refund is initiated, and a cancellation confirmation is returned to the user."},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An order exists in state Assigned (courier assigned but has not picked up)","when":"The order owner requests cancellation","then":"The order transitions to Cancelled, the courier is notified and unassigned, and a refund (minus any applicable cancellation fee) is initiated."},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An order exists in state PickedUp or InTransit (courier has possession of goods)","when":"The order owner requests cancellation","then":"The system rejects the cancellation with a message explaining that the order is already in transit and cannot be cancelled via self-service. The user is directed to customer support."},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An order exists in state Delivered","when":"The order owner requests cancellation","then":"The system rejects the cancellation with a clear message that delivered orders cannot be cancelled."},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An order exists in state Cancelled","when":"Any user requests cancellation of the same order","then":"The system returns an idempotent response indicating the order is already cancelled, with no side effects."},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A valid cancellable order exists","when":"A user who is NOT the order owner requests cancellation","then":"The system rejects the request with an authorization error, and no state change occurs."},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A cancellation request is submitted","when":"The order ID does not exist","then":"The system returns a not-found error."},{"id":"AC-8","given":"Two simultaneous cancellation requests arrive for the same order","when":"Both are processed concurrently","then":"Exactly one succeeds and the order transitions to Cancelled once. The second request receives an idempotent already-cancelled response. No duplicate refunds are issued."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Concurrent cancellation and state advancement (e.g., courier picks up the order at the exact moment user cancels)","expected_behavior":"The system must enforce optimistic locking or equivalent concurrency control. If the order state advanced past the cancellation window before the cancellation commits, the cancellation is rejected. No partial states are allowed."},{"scenario":"User cancels immediately after placing the order (sub-second cancellation)","expected_behavior":"Cancellation succeeds with full refund. The system must handle the case where payment authorization may still be pending — cancellation should void the authorization rather than issue a refund."},{"scenario":"Network failure during cancellation (request sent but response not received)","expected_behavior":"Cancellation must be idempotent. Retrying the same cancellation request produces the same result without duplicate side effects (no double refund, no duplicate courier notifications)."},{"scenario":"Order with multiple items — user wants to cancel only some items","expected_behavior":"Unclear from spec. If partial cancellation is supported, the order remains active with reduced items. If not supported, the system rejects partial cancellation and instructs the user to cancel the entire order. This requires PM clarification."},{"scenario":"Cancelled order's payment refund fails downstream","expected_behavior":"The order remains in Cancelled state (cancellation is not reversed). A failed refund triggers an alert to finance/support for manual resolution. The user is notified that the refund is being processed."},{"scenario":"User cancels an order that has a scheduled pickup time in the future","expected_behavior":"Cancellation succeeds. The scheduled pickup is removed from the courier's queue. Any calendar or routing optimization is updated."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Cancellation directly impacts revenue (lost order value) and may incur refund processing costs. Cancellation fees partially offset courier opportunity cost. Incorrect refund logic (e.g., double refunds from race conditions) poses direct financial risk.","operational":"Courier utilization is affected — cancelled orders after assignment waste courier time. Cancellation rate is a key operational metric; high rates may indicate UX or pricing issues. Support volume decreases when self-service cancellation works correctly.","user":"Cancellation capability is a baseline user expectation. Friction-free cancellation in early order states builds trust. Blocking cancellation after pickup is industry-standard and generally accepted. Poor cancellation UX (e.g., no clear messaging on why cancellation is rejected) drives support tickets and negative reviews.","risk":"Primary risks: (1) Race conditions causing double refunds or orphaned courier assignments, (2) Missing audit trail complicating dispute resolution, (3) Overly permissive cancellation window allowing cancellation of in-transit orders causing courier confusion and lost packages.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"End Users (Order Owners)","impact":"Gain self-service cancellation capability, reducing reliance on support channels. Must receive clear feedback on cancellation eligibility."},{"group":"Couriers","impact":"Must be promptly notified when assigned orders are cancelled to avoid wasted trips. Cancellation after pickup creates operational disruption."},{"group":"Finance / Accounting","impact":"Must reconcile refunds against cancellations. Need reliable audit trail. Must handle edge cases like failed refunds."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Handles escalations for orders that cannot be self-service cancelled (in-transit). Needs visibility into cancellation history for dispute resolution."},{"group":"Product / Analytics","impact":"Cancellation rate and cancellation-by-state distribution are key product health metrics."}]},"confidence":{"level":45,"reasoning":"The PM statement is a single sentence with no specification of cancellation windows, refund policy, partial cancellation support, cancellation fees, or which order states exist in the system. Multiple critical business decisions are left unstated. The acceptance criteria above represent industry-standard defaults for a logistics platform, but several could legitimately differ based on this specific business's policies.","high_confidence_areas":["Cancelled is a terminal state","Only the order owner can cancel their own order","Delivered orders cannot be cancelled","Cancellation requires an audit trail","Idempotency is required for cancellation requests"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact cancellation window cutoff — is it at PickedUp or InTransit or some other state?","Refund policy by state — full refund vs partial refund vs cancellation fee amounts","Whether partial order cancellation (individual items) is supported","Whether there is a time-based cancellation window independent of order state","Whether admin/support agents can cancel on behalf of users (spec says 'users')"],"assumptions":["The order lifecycle includes states: Placed, Confirmed, Assigned, PickedUp, InTransit, Delivered, Cancelled","Cancellation is blocked once the courier has physical possession of goods","Refund is expected on cancellation but policy details are TBD","Only the ordering user (not admin or support) is in scope per the PM statement"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The PM statement 'Users should be able to cancel their order' does not specify which order states permit cancellation. Is cancellation allowed after courier assignment? After pickup? Is there a time-based window (e.g., cancel within 5 minutes regardless of state)?","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Couriers","Finance"],"options":["Allow cancellation only before courier assignment (most restrictive)","Allow cancellation before courier pickup (moderate — industry standard)","Allow cancellation at any pre-delivery state including in-transit (most permissive, operationally complex)"],"recommendation":"Allow cancellation before courier pickup (Placed, Confirmed, Assigned states). Block self-service cancellation once PickedUp. This is the industry standard for courier platforms and balances user flexibility with operational feasibility."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Refund policy on cancellation is unspecified. Should cancellation always trigger a full refund? Should there be a cancellation fee after courier assignment? What happens to promotional credits or discounts?","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Finance","Customer Support"],"options":["Full refund regardless of state at cancellation","Full refund before assignment, partial refund (minus cancellation fee) after assignment","No refund after assignment, full refund only before"],"recommendation":"Full refund before courier assignment. Refund minus a defined cancellation fee after courier assignment but before pickup. This compensates couriers for wasted time while remaining user-friendly."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Partial cancellation (cancelling individual items within a multi-item order) is not addressed. This significantly affects data model and refund logic.","affected_stakeholders":["End Users","Engineering","Finance"],"options":["Support only full order cancellation (simpler, ship faster)","Support partial cancellation with per-item refunds (more flexible, more complex)"],"recommendation":"Start with full order cancellation only. Partial cancellation can be a follow-up feature once the base cancellation flow is validated."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Users' is undefined — does this mean only the order-placing customer, or also admin users and customer support agents acting on behalf of users?","affected_stakeholders":["Customer Support","End Users"],"options":["Only the order owner can cancel (strict self-service)","Order owner and support agents can cancel (broader, requires role-based access)"],"recommendation":"Implement for order owner only in v1. Support-initiated cancellation is a separate workflow with different audit and refund approval requirements."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}

The PM statement 'Users should be able to cancel their order' is underspecified across multiple critical dimensions: which order states allow cancellation, refund policy, partial cancellation, a

content.thinking:
The PM says "Users should be able to cancel their order." This is vague - no details about order states, time windows, refund policies, partial cancellations, etc. This is ambiguity.

Let me produce a thorough domain analysis.
